reasoning: Databricks raised a massive $10B funding round in December 2024 at a $62B valuation, allowing it to provide liquidity to employees and shareholders without having to go public immediately. CEO Ali Ghodsi stated that going public is much less important now than in the past, though an IPO in late 2025 or 2026 remains a possibility. Given the knowledge cutoff is late May 2025 and there have been no credible rumors, leaks, or announcements of an S-1 filing, the window for a pre-July 2025 announcement is very small (less than 6 weeks). Companies typically target fall or spring windows for IPOs; if Databricks were aiming for a summer debut, we would likely have seen an S-1 filing or a public announcement of a confidential filing by mid-May. The lack of any such developments strongly suggests they are waiting until at least the second half of 2025 or 2026. Therefore, the chance of an announcement prior to July 1, 2025, is very low.
